phenomenology_atlas.cancellation: Einstein-identity cancellation theorem at specific x (E1 Lean)
no global cancellation; matter and geometry both grow
phenomenology_atlas.asymptotic: R → 0 at peak as σ → ∞; R → ∞ as σ → 0; R → ∞ at fixed x as σ → 0

phenomenology_atlas.R_sign: strictly positive, constant — 'de Sitter-like' emergent geometry
phenomenology_atlas.spreading_behavior: frozen at any σ_const (admits constant-σ solution)
phenomenology_atlas.asymptotic: R → 0 as σ → ∞ (vacuum); R → ∞ as σ → 0

phenomenology_atlas.domain: x ∈ ℝ  (r > 0 everywhere, for any α, β)
phenomenology_atlas.R_sign: zero everywhere — exact pointwise matter/geometry cancellation
phenomenology_atlas.asymptotic: no σ-dependence; R = 0 for all α, β

phenomenology_atlas.R_form: R = 2·(B/A)²  (CONSTANT in x, depends only on ratio)

lean_obstruction: no_isLocalMax_for_constant_negative_R  (IsLocalMax bridge: no local maximum can host R < 0)
no_global_smooth_bounded_negative_R    (Taylor proof: globally bounded r ∈ [m, M] cannot host R < 0)

summary_remarks.complete_R_classification: R = 0 (E4 exponential, witness), R > 0 (E-Gen-Sech, witness), R < 0 (impossible globally — Taylor proof)
summary_remarks.dynamic_consequence: All constant-R profiles admit frozen (σ ≡ const) solutions, impossible in free Schrödinger.  Backreaction enables vacuum and 'de Sitter-like' emergent geometries.
summary_remarks.optimal_spreading_width: σ̇ = C·σ·exp(−σ) is maximized at σ = 1.  This identifies a characteristic LSBridge length scale at which spreading is fastest.
